name: Reed Piano
explanation: A polyphonic electric reed-piano instrument modelled on the warm 60s struck-steel-reed school pianos. Each voice is a hollow reed-tone oscillator run through an asymmetric soft clipper whose drive tracks velocity and a fast 'bark' envelope, so hard hits growl and bark on the attack then mellow into a hollow sustain — distinct from a tine Rhodes. A snappy amp envelope with adjustable body decay, a one-pole tone tilt and a built-in sine tremolo finish the voice. Up to 12 voices, so chords ring out. Controls: Bark, Decay, Tone, Tremolo Depth, Tremolo Rate and Level.
